title: Refinement Cycles
concept: Repeating a task internally with critique feedback before presenting the final result to the environment or user.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, what does a self-critique prompt ask a model to do?

options.text: Find flaws or hidden assumptions in its own previous answer
Delete the conversation and start over silently
Copy the user's question word for word

exercises.explain: A self-critique prompt tells the model to look back at its own reasoning and spot mistakes or assumptions it made. That honest self-check is the first step toward improving an answer.

cards.title: One draft is rarely the best draft
cards.body: Great writers rewrite. AI agents can do the same trick. Instead of handing over a first attempt, an agent can quietly redo the work a few times to make it better.

cards.title: What is a refinement cycle?
cards.body: A refinement cycle is when an agent does a task, critiques its own result, and redoes it, over and over, before showing the final answer. All this looping happens internally, hidden from you.

cards.title: Like sketching before the final painting
cards.body: An artist draws rough sketches, steps back, fixes what looks off, then paints the real version. You only see the finished piece, not the messy sketches behind it.

cards.title: Polishing an email
cards.body: An agent writes an email draft, critiques it ('too long, unclear ask'), rewrites it shorter and clearer, checks again, then sends only the polished version to you.

cards.title: Myth: more loops always means better
cards.body: Not true! After a few rounds, extra loops stop helping and just waste time. A smart agent knows when the answer is 'good enough' and stops.

cards.title: First try vs. refined result
cards.body: The difference is the invisible loop of critique and redo before you ever see the answer.

left.text: Agent shows its very first draft, flaws and all.

right.text: Agent loops, fixes flaws, then shows the polished version.

exercises.prompt: What best describes a refinement cycle?

options.text: Repeating a task with critique feedback before showing the final result
Asking the user to fix the answer themselves
Giving the fastest possible answer with no checking

exercises.explain: A refinement cycle means the agent repeats the work, using its own critique as feedback, and only presents the result once it has been improved. The looping is internal and done before you see anything.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In a refinement cycle, all the critique and redo steps happen internally before the final answer is shown.
exercises.explain: True. The whole point is that the agent quietly loops through critique and improvement behind the scenes, then presents only the polished final result to the user or environment.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about when the loop stops.
exercises.sentence: A smart agent stops the loop when the answer is good enough, so extra cycles do not waste time.
exercises.explain: The agent should keep refining only while it helps. Once the answer is 'good enough', continuing just wastes time without improving the result.

exercises.prompt: Which description sounds like an AI-generated explanation of a refinement cycle?

options.text: A refinement cycle is an iterative process in which an agent generates an output, evaluates it against internal criteria, and revises before finalizing.
umm its like when the robot just tries once and sends it lol
idk my teacher said loops are boring so i skipped that part

exercises.explain: The first option is polished, structured, and precise, using formal phrasing like 'iterative process' and 'internal criteria'. That clean, complete style is a signal of AI-generated text.

exercises.prompt: An agent is asked to summarize a report. It writes a summary, critiques it as 'missing the key conclusion', rewrites it, critiques again as 'good and complete', and stops. Then it shows you the summary. Which statement is correct?

options.text: The agent used a refinement cycle: it critiqued and redid the work internally, then stopped when it was good enough and presented only the final version.
The agent failed because it should keep looping forever to be safe.
The agent showed you every messy draft, which is what refinement means.
The agent skipped critique entirely and just guessed.

exercises.explain: This is a textbook refinement cycle: do, critique, redo, repeat, then stop when the result is good enough, and present only the polished final answer. It combines self-critique (finding flaws) with looping to improve quality, and it knows when to stop instead of wasting extra cycles.
